content.claim: Hungary's early tracking system (grades 5 and 7 entry points via centralized entrance exams) is associated with very high between-school socio-economic segregation, among the highest in OECD tracked systems.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Segregation magnitude is contested; direction of causation (tracking causes segregation vs. tracking correlates with residential sorting and free school choice) is debated among researchers
World Bank comparison uses Latin American reference countries, which may not be policy-relevant comparators
Cross-national PISA data does not isolate Hungary-specific structural factors
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
